headline: Software Engineering · Systems Thinking · ML/AI · Creative Technical Work
paragraphs: I am applying for the C++ Software Engineer role because Gameloft Barcelona sits exactly at the intersection where my profile makes the most sense: software engineering, creative systems, performance constraints, game feel, visual culture, and the kind of technical curiosity that keeps growing when the domain is difficult and alive.

I know this role asks for proven full-time C++ experience, and I do not want to disguise that gap. What I can offer is a rare junior profile with strong software-engineering foundations, a fast learning curve, and a real motivation for game engineering rather than a generic interest in another programming job. I am completing Software Engineering at UOC with a Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence specialization, and my work combines backend architecture, Java/Spring, SQL, Python, Linux, Git, LaTeX tooling, automation, technical documentation, and repository-backed workflows.

Games are one of the few software domains where aesthetics, player psychology, physics, UX, architecture, tooling, and low-level constraints meet inside the same loop. That is the environment where I learn fastest. My background is unusual: I bring artistic sensibility, strong abstraction skills, comfort moving across different technical topics, and the ability to reason about mechanics and systems from both the player-facing and implementation-facing sides.

For Asphalt Legends Unite, I would not approach the work as isolated feature delivery. A racing game client needs discipline around responsiveness, iteration cost, maintainability, profiling awareness, and cross-functional collaboration with design, art, UI/UX, engine, LiveOps, and other library owners. I am especially interested in that middle ground between gameplay components, technical architecture, tools, and the details that make a feature feel sharp and playable instead of merely functional.

I am not asking Gameloft to ignore the experience requirement. I am asking for the chance to be evaluated as a high-upside engineering profile who can close the C++ production gap with focus, humility, and intensity, while bringing a wider creative and systems perspective than a standard entry-level candidate with a high sensibility for art, culture and psychology.
closing: I would welcome the opportunity to discuss whether my profile could be useful to the Asphalt Legends Unite team and how I could ramp into Gameloft Barcelona's C++ engineering standards.
public_note: Prepared for the Gameloft Barcelona C++ Software Engineer application.
parser_summary: Dídac Llorens; Barcelona; Software Engineering and Computer Science at UOC; Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence specialization; software engineering foundations; systems thinking; creative technical profile; game industry motivation; C++ learning path; object-oriented programming; Java; Spring Boot; SQL; Python; Linux; Git; technical documentation; automation; LaTeX tooling; agentic workflows; architecture; gameplay systems interest; low-level optimization interest; cross-functional collaboration; Plastic Arts and Design qualified; UI/UX; engine-adjacent work; LiveOps awareness.
